title: Perceptrons: An Introduction to Computational Geometry
summary: A rigorous mathematical analysis demonstrating that single-layer perceptrons cannot compute certain functions, most famously XOR. While technically correct and mathematically important, the book was widely interpreted as a death sentence for all neural network research — an overreaction that the authors may or may not have intended. The resulting freeze in neural network funding lasted over a decade.
key_contributions: Proved formal limitations of single-layer perceptrons
Demonstrated that linearly inseparable problems (XOR) cannot be solved by perceptrons
Established computational geometry as a tool for analyzing neural networks
Inadvertently triggered a decade-long freeze in neural network research
impact: One of the most consequential publications in AI history — not for what it proved, but for the chilling effect it had on an entire research paradigm. Multi-layer networks could solve the problems identified, but funding dried up before this was widely demonstrated.
query_hooks: Why did neural network research stop?
What are the limitations of perceptrons?
What caused the first AI winter?
